bird.name: Australian painted-snipe
bird.summary: The Australian painted-snipe (Rostratula australis) is a medium-sized, long-billed, distinctively patterned wader. Taxonomy The distinctive…

relations.subject: Australian painted-snipe
relations.evidence: Causes of the decline are ascribed to wetland drainage, river management and salinisation, as well as grazing and trampling of wetlands by stock
relations.object_summary: 湿地被排干转为农业或建设用地。

relations.subject: Australian painted-snipe
relations.evidence: As of 2022, the global population is estimated to be 270 – 410 mature individuals. Because of this very small population size, the species is listed as Endangered by the IUCN. In Australia it is classified as being nationally threatened with a rating of Endangered
relations.object_summary: 种群规模过小导致遗传多样性下降和脆弱性上升。
